steps.label: Power Series
steps.bodyHtml:
A series of the form
  \[\displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}c_nx^n=c_0+c_1x+c_2x^2+\cdots\]is a power series centered at \(x=0\). A series of the form
  \[\displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}c_n(x-a)^n=c_0+c_1(x-a)+c_2(x-a)^2+\cdots\]is a power series centered at \(x=a\). We are defining that \(x^0=1\) and \((x-a)^0=1\) even when x=0 and x=a respectively.
